raw_consumer_statement:
I am disputing the remaining {$700.00} from a {$1000.00} engine purchase from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/year>, made with my Citi / AAdvantage MileUp card. The engine arrived damaged, and I provided photo evidence. The merchant accepted the return but withheld {$700.00}, claiming a restocking fee that does not apply to defective merchandise. 

Citi has not fulfilled my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ). I submitted and sent out in mailnew evidence, including photos and communication showing the part was defective and that the restocking fee should not apply. Citi did not conduct a proper investigation of this new evidence. 

Citis denial letter indicates the dispute team relied solely on the merchants statement and terms and conditions, without performing a manual review of the evidence I sent. They did not address the defect, did not explain why the restocking fee applies to defective merchandise, and did not evaluate the factual proof I provided. 

This is a failure to conduct a reasonable, good-faith investigation, as required under the FCBA for disputes involving defective or non-conforming goods. 

Details : Total purchase : {$1000.00} XXXX issued only a {$340.00} credit, keeping {$700.00} as a restocking fee This fee was not disclosed verbally and does not apply to broken items I returned the item because it was damaged and unusable Citi closed the dispute without properly reviewing my evidence
